description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a brushed metal front panel and wooden side panels, giving it a classic and robust appearance. The front face features a large horizontal frequency display illuminated in blue, a prominent tuning knob on the right, and several smaller knobs and switches for volume, balance, and other controls. There is also a small analog meter on the left side of the front panel, likely for signal strength or tuning accuracy. The top surface has ventilation slots, suggesting internal electronic components that require cooling. The rear panel is equipped with multiple RCA input/output ports and speaker terminals, indicating its use as an audio receiver or amplifier. The overall design is rectangular and boxy, with a combination of metallic and wood textures that evoke mid-20th-century audio equipment.
description: A vintage radio receiver with a brushed metal front, wooden sides, blue-lit frequency display, and multiple control knobs and ports. The design is rectangular and classic, with both front and rear interactive features.
description_view_0: The front view shows the frequency display, analog meter, large tuning knob, and several smaller control knobs and switches.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the wooden panel and the protruding control knobs, with no additional features visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals multiple RCA input/output ports, speaker terminals, and ventilation slots on the top.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the wooden panel and the ends of the control knobs, similar to the left side.
